sentence_reviews.text: Context encoders predict abstract target embeddings, emphasizing semantic and dynamical structure rather than pixel-perfect reconstruction.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Assran et al. 2023 JEPA paper/abstract describes predicting target-block representations from context blocks and avoiding pixel-level reconstruction.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for image JEPA: context predicts target representations rather than reconstructing pixels.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Predictive representation learning / JEPA, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Joint-embedding predictive architectures follows the learning setup expressed by Predictive representation learning / JEPA.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Assran et al. 2023 is the cited joint-embedding predictive architecture source.
sentence_reviews.notes: The node is an instance of the cited JEPA learning setup.

sentence_reviews.text: Joint-embedding predictive architectures consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Assran et al. 2023 describes context and target representations/embeddings.
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence directly supports representation/embedding use.

sentence_reviews.text: Joint-embedding predictive architectures is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Assran et al. 2023 describes predicting masked target blocks from a context block.
sentence_reviews.notes: Masking/target-block selection is directly part of I-JEPA.

sentence_reviews.text: Joint-embedding predictive architectures extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Assran et al. 2023 uses a vision-transformer style architecture; Vaswani et al. 2017 defines transformers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported for I-JEPA implementations, but JEPA as an architectural idea need not always specialize transformers.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Joint-embedding predictive architectures is modeled as depending on Predictive representation learning / JEPA, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Robotics & embodied AI, Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: JEPA is directly the cited predictive learning setup; core trained_under edge is supported.
Context/target embeddings are central, supporting a core represents_with edge.
Masked context/target block prediction directly supports a common composes edge.
I-JEPA uses transformer-style encoders, but the architecture-family edge is implementation-specific rather than inherent to all JEPA formulations.
